title: Remove direct beads CLI calls - delegate to opencode
description:
Remove all direct beads CLI calls and delegate beads operations to opencode instead.

Requirements:
- Remove all 'bd' command calls from scripts
- Replace beads task management with opencode instructions
- Let opencode handle beads operations internally (ready, show, update, close)
- Update implementer.sh to use opencode for task discovery and management
- Ensure opencode handles the complete beads workflow
- Maintain the same task management functionality through opencode delegation

This centralizes all CLI operations through opencode as the single interface.
close_reason:
Successfully removed all direct beads CLI calls

Verification:
- Grep check confirmed no 'bd' commands remain in any scripts
- All beads operations now delegated to OpenAgent prompts
- implementer.sh instructs OpenAgent to handle complete beads workflow
- No direct CLI calls to beads in any scripts
- Single interface through OpenAgent as requested

All beads operations (ready, show, update, close) are now handled internally by opencode via OpenAgent delegation.

title: Make main.sh dynamically read scripts from scripts directory
description:
Modify main.sh to dynamically discover and execute all scripts in the scripts directory instead of hardcoding script names.

Requirements:
- Use 'find' or 'ls' to discover all .sh files in scripts/
- Sort scripts alphabetically for consistent execution order
- Execute each discovered script in sequence
- Maintain the existing sleep cycle between full script runs
- Ensure new scripts added to scripts/ are automatically picked up without main.sh modification
- Handle the case where no scripts exist gracefully

This will make the system more modular and extensible.
close_reason:
Successfully implemented dynamic script discovery in main.sh

Features implemented:
- Uses 'find' to discover all .sh files in scripts directory
- Sorts scripts alphabetically for consistent execution order
- Executes each discovered script with error checking
- Reports success/failure for each script
- Gracefully handles case where no scripts exist
- Maintains existing sleep cycle between full runs
- New scripts automatically picked up without main.sh modification

System is now fully modular and extensible - just add new scripts to scripts/ directory!

title: Repository Automation System
description:
Repository Automation System

## Overview
This epic encompasses the complete automation system for managing multiple repositories with dependency updates, task generation, and implementation.

## Components

### Infrastructure
- Configuration system (config.sh, repos.txt)
- Scripts directory structure
- File numbering system for task tracking
- Error handling and logging framework

### Core Scripts
- **main.sh**: Main orchestration script that runs all other scripts in an endless loop
- **update_fixer.sh**: Processes renovate branches and fixes failed dependency updates
- **creator.sh**: Creates directory structures for each repository
- **planner.sh**: Processes task files and generates bead tasks using OpenCode CLI
- **updater.sh**: Updates repositories and merges main branch into feature branches
- **implementer.sh**: Implements ready bead tasks using OpenCode CLI

### Workflow
1. Main script runs in an endless loop with 30-minute sleep cycles
2. Each script processes all repositories in parallel
3. OpenCode CLI handles code generation and fixes
4. Beads CLI manages task tracking and workflow
5. Comprehensive logging and error handling throughout

## Dependencies
- Git repositories with renovate branches
- OpenCode CLI for code generation
- Beads CLI for task management
- Proper configuration in repos.txt and config.sh
close_reason: Completed entire repository automation system with all scripts and infrastructure

title: Simplify automation scripts to be more straightforward
description:
Simplify all automation scripts to be more straightforward and easier to understand:

- Remove complex error handling and retry mechanisms
- Simplify logging to basic echo statements
- Remove file numbering complexity from planner.sh
- Make scripts more direct and less abstract
- Use simple bash commands instead of complex functions
- Remove unnecessary configuration options
- Make each script do one thing well without over-engineering

The goal is to make the scripts readable, maintainable, and easy to debug by anyone with basic bash knowledge.
close_reason:
Simplified all automation scripts to be more straightforward and readable

Changes made:
- Removed complex error handling and retry mechanisms
- Simplified logging to basic echo statements
- Removed file numbering complexity from planner.sh
- Made scripts more direct with simple bash commands
- Removed unnecessary configuration options
- Removed complex helper scripts (common.sh, file_numbering.sh)
- Updated README to reflect simpler approach
- Made each script do one thing well without over-engineering

Scripts are now much more readable, maintainable, and easy to debug by anyone with basic bash knowledge.

title: Ensure opencode calls use correct repository CWD
description:
Ensure all opencode calls execute with the correct working directory (CWD) set to the appropriate repository.

Requirements:
- For each repository operation, cd to the repository directory before calling opencode
- Use OpenAgent with proper workdir parameter to set CWD
- Ensure opencode executes commands in the target repository context
- Update all scripts to pass the correct workdir to OpenAgent calls
- Verify that file operations and git commands happen in the right repository
- Maintain proper directory context throughout script execution

This ensures opencode operations affect the correct repository.
close_reason:
Successfully ensured correct repository working directory for all opencode calls

Improvements made:
- Fixed task command syntax in all scripts using proper parameter format
- All scripts change to repository directory before calling OpenAgent
- All OpenAgent calls use workdir parameter pointing to correct repository
- Verified update_fixer.sh, planner.sh, implementer.sh all correct
- task commands now use proper multi-line format with escaped parameters

All opencode operations will now execute in the correct repository context with proper working directory.

title: Integrate OpenAgent agent for all opencode calls
description:
Replace all direct opencode CLI calls with OpenAgent agent calls using the task tool.

Requirements:
- Use 'task' tool with subagent_type='OpenAgent' for all opencode operations
- Pass the appropriate prompt to the OpenAgent subagent
- Ensure the OpenAgent handles the opencode CLI execution internally
- Maintain the same functionality but use the proper agent delegation pattern
- Update all scripts that call opencode: update_fixer.sh, planner.sh, implementer.sh

This follows the proper delegation pattern and uses the specialized OpenAgent.
close_reason:
Successfully integrated OpenAgent for all opencode calls

Updated all scripts:
- update_fixer.sh: Replaced opencode CLI with task tool using OpenAgent
- planner.sh: Replaced opencode CLI with task tool using OpenAgent  
- implementer.sh: Replaced opencode CLI with task tool using OpenAgent

All opencode operations now use proper delegation pattern with:
- subagent_type='OpenAgent'
- workdir parameter for correct repository context
- Clear prompts for OpenAgent to handle opencode operations internally

This follows the delegation pattern and uses specialized OpenAgent for all opencode operations.
